questions.question: What is the primary function of the BIOS?
options: C) To manage booting and hardware configuration
questions.explanation: The BIOS (Basic Input/Output System) is a firmware that controls the booting process and manages the configuration of the hardware components of a computer.

questions.question: Which of the following operating systems is an example of a single-user, single-tasking system?
questions.explanation: Single-user, single-tasking systems are designed to run a single application at a time and allow only one user to access the system.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the kernel in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation and process scheduling
B) To manage file systems and input/output operations
C) To manage booting and hardware configuration
questions.explanation: The kernel is the central part of an operating system that manages the system's resources and provides services to applications.

questions.question: Which of the following types of memory is volatile?
questions.explanation: Volatile memory loses its data when the power is turned off, whereas non-volatile memory retains its data even after the power is turned off.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the device driver in an operating system?
options: A) To manage memory allocation and process scheduling
B) To manage file systems and input/output operations
C) To manage booting and hardware configuration
D) To manage interactions between hardware devices and the operating system
questions.explanation: Device drivers are software components that enable the operating system to communicate with hardware devices and manage their interactions.
